Partial competency. Notable gaps persist. The pilot needs improvement before operational validation.
3
STANDARD
Expected level. The pilot demonstrates the competency consistently and without intervention. This is the norm, not mediocrity.
4
ABOVE STANDARD
Clearly superior performance. The pilot anticipates, shares, adapts. Exemplary behaviors on multiple aspects of the competency.
5
EXEMPLARY
Exceptional and consistent mastery. The pilot elevates the crew's level. Reserved for truly remarkable performances — rare.
⚠️ THE 5 BIASES THAT DISTORT A GRADE
HALO EFFECT
A likeable or reputedly good pilot receives 4s everywhere even if a competency was insufficient that day. Grade what you SAW, not what you think of them.
RECENCY BIAS
You only grade the last 5 minutes of the exercise. A good landing doesn't compensate 20 minutes of degraded WLM. Grade over the full duration.
SYSTEMATIC LENIENCY
Giving 3 by default to avoid conflict or not discourage. A 3 must be earned — if you have not observed the standard, put 2. Kindness does not protect the pilot.
EXCESSIVE SEVERITY
Never putting 4 or 5 "to avoid making them arrogant". A well-deserved 4 motivates and calibrates the pilot. Refusing to grade high is also an error.
CONTRAST BIAS
You just assessed a very poor pilot — the next one seems excellent by comparison. Each pilot is graded against the absolute CBTA reference, not against others.
👁 THE OBSERVABLE RULE
An observable must be seen, heard or measured. Never an interpretation or a feeling.
✗
"Poor situational awareness"
✓
"PM did not announce FL100 crossing — 200ft deviation not verbalised for 40 seconds"
✗
"He can't make decisions"
✓
"Decision to continue approach taken unilaterally — diversion option never raised, FORDEC not applied"
📱 HOW TO GRADE IN THE APP — STEP BY STEP
1
Open the exercise → EVALUATION tab
2
Select CPT or FO via the switcher at the top
3
Tap the relevant competency (e.g. PSD) → the grading panel opens
4
Tap the grade 1 to 5 — the grade color lights up
5
Tap + OBS to add a positive observable or − OBS for negative
6
Write the observable as observed behavior — what you saw or heard, not your interpretation
7
Switch to FO → repeat steps 3–6 for the co-pilot
🤖 SUGGESTED GRADING — HOW IT WORKS
After checking observables, the app displays a suggested grade in orange. This is not an automatic decision — it is a starting point for your reflection. You are the only one to judge.
TWO MODES DEPENDING ON HOW YOU GRADE:
MODE 1 — You only note deficiencies
Fast workflow: you only check what went wrong (the "−"). Unchecked behaviors are considered acceptable.
FORMULA
score = (total official OB − nb of "−") ÷ total OB
Example — PSD competency (9 official OBs):
1 deficiency (9−1)÷9 = 89% 4 Above
3 deficiencies (9−3)÷9 = 67% 4 Above
5 deficiencies (9−5)÷9 = 44% 2 Below standard
9 deficiencies (9−9)÷9 = 0% 1 Unacceptable
MODE 2 — You note good AND bad behaviors
Detailed workflow: you check "+" and "−". The calculation is based solely on what you actively noted.
FORMULA
score = nb of "+" ÷ (nb of "+" + nb of "−")
Example — PSD competency:
7+ and 1− 7÷8 = 87% 4 Above
5+ and 3− 5÷8 = 62% 4 Above
3+ and 5− 3÷8 = 37% 2 Below standard
CONVERSION TABLE (% → GRADE)
≥ 90%5EXEMPLARY
≥ 70%4ABOVE STANDARD
≥ 50%3STANDARD
≥ 30%2BELOW STANDARD
< 30%1UNACCEPTABLE
⚖️ THE INSTRUCTOR IS ALWAYS MASTER OF GRADING
The suggested grade is an aid tool, not a decision. The algorithm counts checkmarks — it does not see the situation, the pilot's energy, the scenario complexity, nor the CRM context. You do.
✗
Never tap "APPLY" without thinking — read the suggestion, compare to your overall impression.
✓
Use the suggestion as an anchor point: if it says 4 and you were hesitating between 3 and 4, it confirms 4. If it says 3 but the pilot was clearly struggling, put 2.
✓
A single dangerous behavior can justify a 1 even if other observables were positive — safety takes precedence over the average.
✈️ FULL EXAMPLE — SE ILS (SINGLE ENGINE ILS)
Exercise: engine failure after V1, single-engine ILS approach, go-around
KNO3ENG FIRE procedure correct — 1 item missing (FUEL CROSS FEED)
WLM4Load well distributed — PM managed radio while PF flew
SA3Situational awareness maintained — destination weather not checked
PSD2FORDEC not applied — go-around decision taken unilaterally without consulting PM
LTW3Correct coordination — PM not briefed before GA (no NITS)
FPM4ILS track and glide path precise throughout — deviations < ½ dot
💡 DEBRIEF READING
The 3.2 average reflects a technically solid pilot but with a PSD/LTW deficit. The debrief focuses on FORDEC and NITS — not on technique which is at standard.
📡 CHAPTER 12 — OFFLINE MODE
The app works 100% offline after the first visit with a connection — ideal for simulator environments where wifi is unavailable or unreliable.
⚡ ACTIVATION — ONE TIME ONLY
1
Open the app once with a connection
Visit the GitHub Pages URL once on Safari (iPhone) or Chrome (Android). The app caches automatically in the background — this is silent and automatic.
✅ IN THE SIMULATOR — NO CONNECTION NEEDED
✓
Turn off wifi or enable Airplane Mode — the app opens exactly as normal
✓
All features available: exercises, grading, debrief, guide
✓
Session data saved locally on device — never lost
✓
PDF generation and export work fully offline
🧪 TEST BEFORE THE EXAM DAY
1. Open app with wifi
2. Disable wifi
3. Close and reopen the app
→ If it loads = offline operational ✓
🔄 AUTOMATIC UPDATES
When a new version is published, the app updates silently in the background on the next connection. No action required.
🎯 CHAPTER 13 — ADVANCED INSTRUCTOR MONITORING
📊 INTERVENTION MODEL TRACKER
A permanent intervention bar visible during every exercise. Records each instructor action with a timestamp and exercise name.
🟢 ASKQuestion posed to the trainee — soft intervention
🟠 RECVerbal recommendation — active guidance
🔴 CTLTaking control — physical intervention
→ Counters are updated in real time. The Events tab in the debrief shows the full timeline.
🚩 EVENT FLAG
Instantly marks a key moment during the session with an optional note. Ideal for flagging an error, a good decision, or any event to revisit during the debrief.
Usage: Tap 🚩 FLAG → enter a quick note (optional) → MARK. The flag is saved with elapsed time and the current exercise name.
🎙 VOICE NOTES
Hands-free voice dictation during the session. The app uses the device's built-in speech recognition (Safari/Chrome). Transcribed text is saved automatically with a timestamp.
Usage: Tap 🎙 VOICE → speak → the app transcribes and saves. Tap again to stop. Note: requires microphone permission on the device.
📝 CONCLUSION TEMPLATES
5 pre-written conclusion templates available in the Conclusion tab of the debrief. One tap applies the text to the free-note area.
EXEMPLARY ABOVE STANDARD STANDARD BELOW STANDARD UNACCEPTABLE
→ The text can be freely edited after applying a template.
🏥 CHAPTER 14 — EBT, CBTA & ADVANCED CLINICAL TOOLS
⚖️ CBTA VS EBT — WHAT IS THE DIFFERENCE?
📐 CBTA
Competency-Based Training & Assessment
ICAO regulatory framework. Training is structured around demonstrable competencies rather than flight hours. We assess what the pilot can do, not how long they spent doing it.
CBTA question: "Does the pilot demonstrate SA competency at level 4?"
🔬 EBT
Evidence-Based Training
Boeing/IATA methodology. Training scenarios are built from real safety data (accidents, incidents, FOQA, ASAP). We train on what kills, not on what is easy.
EBT question: "Which competencies failed in the last 10 similar accidents?"
Summary: CBTA is the general framework (assess by competencies) — EBT is the method that drives which scenarios to choose and which behaviors to observe. This app uses the 9 CBTA competencies with EBT logic: behavioral indicators (BI) come from Boeing accident data.
🎓 HOW TO USE — COMPLETE WORKFLOW
1
PRE-SESSION BRIEFING
Set EBT objectives (MAINTAIN/ACQUIRE/IMPROVE) per competency. Ex: WLM=IMPROVE if the pilot tends to saturate under high workload. Enter weather data → the app auto-computes the Boeing FCT limit and LVO category.
2
DURING THE EXERCISE
Use the intervention buttons (ASK/REC/CTL/FLAG) to log your actions in real time. In the Evaluation tab → check the behavioral indicators (BI) observed. Ex: for SA, check "Announces time constraints" if you see it.
3
DEBRIEF — LOFT TAB
Phase 2: show the self-assessment page to the trainee (1→5 per competency). Press "SHOW MY GRADES" → side-by-side comparison. Red cells (gap ≥ 2) are your priority debrief points.
4
DEBRIEF — CRM TAB
Check FORDEC and NITS rates. Green ≥80% = good habit. Red <40% = area for work. Ex: "You only applied FORDEC on 1 of 4 exercises with a critical decision — insufficient for a check."
5
EXAMINER'S REPORT
Rapport tab → global result SATISFACTORY/UNSATISFACTORY, competency scores, ATPL + OPS boxes. PDF print button for the Boeing file.
6
HISTORY — TNA
Pilot profile → TNA: analysis of the last 5 sessions. If SA is in deficit across 3 consecutive sessions → identified training need. Plan a targeted SA LOFT exercise.
🎯 SESSION OBJECTIVES — EBT PER PILOT
In the briefing, two side-by-side panels — one for CPT, one for FO. Each pilot has independent objectives across the 9 CBTA competencies.
MAINTAIN
Competency is at level — ensure it stays stable
ACQUIRE
New competency — first real exposure
IMPROVE
Competency in deficit — targeted development session
NOT SET
No specific objective for this competency
📊 AUTO RESULTS IN DEBRIEF (SCORES tab):
ACHIEVED ✓Score ≥ 3.5 — objective met
PARTIAL ⚡Score 3.0–3.4 — progressing but incomplete
NOT ACHIEVED ✗Score < 3.0 — priority area for next session
→ Ex: CPT with SA=IMPROVE and WLM=IMPROVE · FO with KNO=ACQUIRE and FPA=IMPROVE — two separate results in debrief.
🌬️ WEATHER DATA — ENTRY & FCT LIMITS
The 4 weather input fields are color-coded in neon colors with glow halos for instant identification:
🟢 HEADWIND / TAILWIND
Lime · Max tailwind: 10 kt — automatic red alert if exceeded
🔵 CROSSWIND
Cyan · Red alert if FCT limit exceeded
🌸 CEILING
Rose · Enter in ft · Auto LVO detection
🟩 VISIBILITY / RVR
Green · Enter in m · Auto LVO detection
📊 RCAM — RUNWAY CONDITION ASSESSMENT MATRIX (Boeing FCT 737 NG):
Select mode (TALPA/RCC or Non-TALPA) then runway condition → the XW limit is shown. The full RCAM table is available as a collapsible panel in the app.
RCC
CONDITION
XW T/O
XW LDG
6
Dry runway
36/33*
36/33*
5
GOOD — Frost · Wet · Dry/wet snow
25
36/33*
4
GOOD–MED — Compacted snow ≤-15°C
22
30
3
MEDIUM — Wet/slippery · Snow >3mm
20
25
2
MED–POOR — Water/slush >3mm
15
17
1
POOR — Ice
13
15
0
NIL — Wet ice · Snow on ice
NIL
NIL
* 36/33 kt with winglets · Source: Boeing FCT 737 NG
🔍 LVO AUTO-DETECTION:
NORMAL/CAT IRVR ≥ 550m & ceiling ≥ 200ft
LVP PREPRVR < 550m or ceiling < 200ft
CAT IIRVR < 400m / DH < 100ft
CAT IIIARVR < 300m / DH < 100ft
CAT IIIBRVR < 175m / DH < 50ft
CAT IIICRVR ≈ 0m / No DH
📋 BEHAVIORAL INDICATORS (BI) — HOW IT WORKS
The grade (1→5) says how much the pilot masters — BI says what was concretely observed. Not "SA=3" but "SA=3 because he didn't announce the fuel constraint before the weather degradation".
DURING Exercise → Evaluation → BEHAVIORAL INDICATORS ▾ → check what you SEE for CPT and FO separately. No need to check everything — only observed behaviors.
DEBRIEF SCORES tab → OBSERVED BEHAVIORS section at the bottom. All BIs checked during the session are grouped by competency and pilot.
📖 READING THE BI SUMMARY:
✓Behavior observed at least once
2×Frequency badge — behavior seen in 2+ exercises → recurring strength
KNO 3/53 of 5 KNO behaviors were observed across the session
→ BIs do not change the 1→5 grade — they are your observational evidence to back up grades in the debrief conversation.
🏥 STRUCTURED LOFT DEBRIEF — 6 PHASES
• Phase 1 — Key events: chronological flight summary (free text area).
• Phase 2 — Pilot self-assessment: show the screen to the trainee → they rate themselves (1→5 per competency) → press "SHOW MY GRADES" → side-by-side comparison. Red cells = priority debrief points.
• Phase 3 — TEM analysis: direct link to the TEM tab for threats/errors.
• Phase 4 — CRM analysis: direct link to the CRM tab for FORDEC/NITS rates.
• Phase 5 — 3 Learning points: the 3 concrete takeaways from the session. Formulated jointly with the trainee. Examples: "Anticipate fuel constraint before step-down", "Apply FORDEC before any abnormal decision", "Brief the PM before every go-around". These points become the foundation for the next session.
• Phase 6 — Action plan: concrete actions to prepare before the next session. Examples: "Practice manual approaches without AP", "Review CAT IIIA procedures", "Work on CPT-FO communication during abnormals". This plan is the reference for the next TRI in pilot follow-up.
📄 EXAMINER'S REPORT & DIGITAL SIGNATURES
Rapport tab in the debrief — Boeing/EASA official format with: global result SATISFACTORY/UNSATISFACTORY, exercises table, competency scores, instructor remarks.
✍️ Digital signature pads (finger or stylus):
TRI / INST.Violet — instructor conducting the session
CPTBlue — Captain trainee
FOGreen — First Officer trainee
Signatures are embedded in the printed PDF. CLEAR button available under each pad.
✅ PROFICIENCY CHECK
PROF CHECK button in the navigation bar — standalone form for regulatory checks (OPC/PC). Fill in mandatory, LVO, LOFT and RHS items with grades 1→5. Result: SATISFACTORY / UNSATISFACTORY.
✍️ Proficiency Check digital signatures:
TRI/SFIViolet — examiner conducting the check
CAPT assessedBlue — Captain being evaluated
FO assessedGreen — First Officer being evaluated
TRI CHECKAmber — a TRI being assessed in a TRI Check
Signatures auto-clear when switching position. Embedded in printed PDF.
📊 TRAINING NEEDS ANALYSIS (TNA)
In History → Pilot profile → TNA section. Automatic analysis of the last 5 sessions: priority deficit competencies (red), to consolidate (amber), strengths (green) with targeted training recommendations.
⚡ CHAPTER 15 — QUICK PHRASES
Save time during the session with a library of 120+ standard B737 phrases covering all CBTA competencies. The more you use a phrase, the higher it ranks in suggestions.
📖 HOW IT WORKS
1
Type to auto-complete
Type 2 or more characters in any comment area and matching phrases appear at the bottom of the screen. For example, type good and all phrases containing "good" are suggested.
2
Browse the library
Tap the ⚡ Phrases button next to any text area to open the full library. Filter by category: KNO, PRO, FPA, COM, TEM, etc.
3
Filter: POSITIF / NÉGATIF
Below the categories, three filter buttons appear:
✓ POSITIF — positive comments only (strengths)
TOUT — all phrases mixed
✗ NÉGATIF — improvement areas only
Each phrase is marked with a green dot (positive) or red dot (negative).
4
Multi-select → cumulative insert
Tap a phrase → it is appended to the text area (new line). The library stays open: select as many phrases as needed, both positive and negative. A green ✓ badge marks already-selected phrases. Close with ✕ when done.
📂 12 CATEGORIES AVAILABLE
GENERAL KNO PRO FPA COM LTW PSD SA WLM FPM TEM CONCLUSION
📍 WHERE IT'S AVAILABLE
• Private notes in each exercise (Evaluation tab)
• TEM input — threats, errors, UAS (TEM tab)
• CRM notes overall (CRM section of debrief)
• Debrief notes per root cause analysis
• Conclusion / recommendation (conclusion section)
📋 EXAMPLE — FULL WORKFLOW
ENGINE FIRE exercise completed. Open the ⚡ library in private notes:
1. Tap ✓ POSITIF → select "Good CRM during complex failure management" and "Effective use of QRH procedures".
2. Tap ✗ NÉGATIF → select "Should improve crosscheck during memory items".
3. Close → all 3 phrases are in your notes, each on its own line. Ready for debrief.
💡 PRO TIP
Start with ✗ NÉGATIF to quickly identify improvement areas, then switch to ✓ POSITIF to balance the debrief. Phrases are in standard aviation English for direct use in official CBTA reports.
⚡ CHAPTER 16 — COMPACT MODE
For TRIs who want to see everything at a glance without switching tabs. Ideal during the session when every second counts.
🔓 HOW TO ACCESS
Open any exercise → in the tab bar at the top, tap ⚡ COMPACT
📋 WHAT'S DISPLAYED
⚡ COMPETENCIES
All 9 competencies in a compact 2×5 grid with score and +/− observations. Tap to open detail.
✈ FFA
FLY · FOCUS · ACT with inline ✓/✗ buttons. Evaluate in one tap.
▲ TEM
Threats, errors, UAS listed with one-click mitigation (✓/~/✗). Add items directly.
◆ FORDEC · NITS
Two big toggle buttons. One tap = used/not used.
📝 NOTES
Private notes with direct access to Quick Phrases ⚡.
? FACILITATION
Expandable questions with integrated note areas.
📋 USAGE EXAMPLE
ENGINE FIRE exercise in progress. In Compact mode, you simultaneously see that PRO = 4, FPA = 3, FLY is ✓, and there are 2 managed TEM threats. Add a note at the bottom without leaving the screen. Zero tab navigation.
🎯 CHAPTER 17 — FOCUS MODE
Filters out the noise to show only what needs attention. When you want to see a pilot's weak points at a glance without scrolling through all competencies.
🔓 HOW TO ACCESS
Open any exercise → in the tab bar at the top, tap 🎯 FOCUS
🔍 WHAT'S AUTOMATICALLY DETECTED
!
Critical competencies
Score below 3 (unsatisfactory/sub-standard), KEY competency not yet graded, or competency with negative (−) observations.
▲
Unmanaged TEM
Threats, errors, or UAS marked as ✗ Unmanaged or ~ Partial. Properly managed items are hidden.
✗
Failed FFA steps
FLY, FOCUS, or ACT steps marked as NO — indicating a gap in the failure management sequence.
📊 VISUAL ELEMENTS
• Status banner — red if critical items exist, green if all clear
• Average score — calculated across all graded competencies
• Bar chart — quick overview of all 9 competencies (green/yellow/red)
• Detail cards — each critical competency with its +/− observations
• Compact button — switch to Compact mode in one tap
📋 EXAMPLE
After a TCAS RA exercise, Focus mode displays: PSD = 2 (no TCAS threat detection), 1 unmanaged UAS (altitude deviation), and FLY = ✗ (trajectory not maintained during maneuver). You instantly know what to debrief.
📋 CHAPTER 18 — PILOT PROFILE CARD
A trading card style overlay showing a pilot's complete profile across all past sessions. Radar, scores, trend, evolution — all computed automatically.
🔓 HOW TO ACCESS
1
Enter the matricule
On the home screen, type a pilot's matricule who has been previously evaluated. The badge turns green ✓ 3 SESSIONS.
2
Tap PILOT PROFILE
A blue button 📋 FICHE PILOTE appears below the matricule. Tap it to open the full profile.
📊 CARD CONTENTS
• Identity — Name, matricule, company, role (CPT/FO)
• SCORE logo — Top-right with Share/Close buttons
• Radar SVG — Average of 9 competencies across all sessions
• Global score — With colored stamp (red → blue) and level label
• Trend — ↗ Improving / ↘ Declining / → Stable
• Statistics — Session count, exercise count, last session date
• Competency bars — Each competency with its colored average score
• Strengths / Areas for improvement — Auto-identified
• Evolution chart — Graph of last 8 sessions with score values
📤 SHARING
Tap 📤 to export the card as an HTML file via AirDrop, WhatsApp, or email. The pilot can open it in any browser.
📋 EXAMPLE
CPT matricule 30078 has had 5 sessions over 6 months. His card shows a well-filled radar except for PSD = 2.8 and WLM = 3.1. The trend is ↗ Improving as his last 2 sessions are better. You instantly identify the areas to work on.
📊 CHAPTER 19 — FLEET DASHBOARD
The fleet dashboard goes beyond basic statistics. It provides a consolidated view of all pilots to identify trends, risks and training needs at fleet level.
📖 HOW TO ACCESS
Home screen → 📋 HISTORY → Dashboard tab (first tab, active by default).
📋 DASHBOARD SECTIONS
⚠ PILOTS AT RISK
Auto-detected: pilots with last session score < 3.0, downward trend ▼, or 2+ competencies below standard. Weak competencies shown as colored badges.
🎯 COMPETENCY HEATMAP
Pilots × Competencies matrix with color-coded cells (green=good, red=weak). Spot at a glance which pilots have gaps in which competencies.
📋 FLEET TNA
Aggregated Training Needs Analysis. Competencies flagged CRITICAL, PRIORITY, or MONITOR based on fleet averages and percentage of pilots below standard.
📋 USAGE EXAMPLE
You're a Chief Pilot with 12 TRIs reporting to you. The Fleet Dashboard shows SA and WLM below 3.5 for 25% of the fleet. The TNA flags them as PRIORITY. You schedule a targeted LOFT for next quarter. The heatmap tells you exactly which pilots should attend.
📱 CHAPTER 20 — QR SELF-ASSESSMENT
No need to hand over your iPad. Generate a QR code that the pilot scans with their phone. They self-assess alone, then give you a 9-digit code that auto-imports their scores.
📖 4-STEP WORKFLOW
1
Generate QR code
Debrief → LOFT tab → Phase 2 → tap 📱 QR AUTO-ÉVAL. A QR code appears for each pilot.
2
Trainee scans
Pilot scans the QR with their phone camera. A clean self-assessment page opens: 9 competencies to rate 1-5. No login required.
3
Trainee validates
After rating all 9 competencies, tap ✓ VALIDATE. A 9-digit code appears (one digit per competency). Example: 434543453
4
Import the code
Enter the code in the QR popup input field → press OK. All 9 scores auto-populate Phase 2. Then tap 👁 SHOW MY GRADES to compare.
📌 QUICK REFERENCE
🎨 CBTA GRADES — COLOR CODE
5
EXEMPLARY
Full mastery — fully autonomous
4
ABOVE STANDARD
Good performance, minor corrections
3
REQUIRED STANDARD
Acceptable, AOM compliant
2
BELOW STANDARD
Deficiencies — monitoring required
1
UNACCEPTABLE
Unacceptable performance — remediation required
🧠 THE 9 CBTA COMPETENCIES
FPMFlight Path Management — trajectory & energy manual
FPAFlight Path Automation — AP / AT / FMC / LNAV / VNAV
PROProcedures — SOP, QRH, NNC, memory actions
SASituation Awareness — continuous awareness of the situation
WLMWorkload Management — prioritisation, task delegation
COMCommunication — ATC phraseology, crew exchanges, callouts
LTWLeadership & Teamwork — assertiveness, coordination, cross-monitoring
PSDProblem Solving & Decision — FORDEC, adaptability, cognitive biases
KNOKnowledge — systems, limitations, EASA regulations
⚡ EXPERT LEVEL BADGE
Some exercises are marked ⚡ EXPERT — advanced procedures requiring multi-competency mastery. Ex: ILS RAW DATA, AIRSPEED UNRELIABLE, RNP AR…
🔄 WORKFLOW — 8 STEPS
1
WELCOME — SCORE
① Enter CPT and F/O staff number → name auto-fills if known.
② Roles: CPT / FO / TRI CHECK.
③ Name the session (e.g.: B737 OPC LVO).
④ Optional: 🗂 button to load a Loft.
2
PRE-SESSION BRIEFING
Check pre-flight items, review EASA tolerances, then ✈ START.
3
SESSION — Add exercises
+ EXERCISE → search by name, competency or phase (TAKEOFF CLIMB CRUISE DESCENT APPROACH LANDING).
MANDATORY bar at top = EASA mandatory items.
Start the SESSION timer before the first exercise.
4
EXERCISE — 4 assessment tabs
EVALUATION — Grades 1–5 per competency + observables
FFA — FLY / FOCUS / ACT
TEM — Threats · Errors · UAS · Countermeasures
FACILITATION — Debrief questions
5
T / M — Training or Mandatory
● T = free training · ● M = EASA mandatory.
Failure (<3) → ↻ 2nd ATTEMPT button appears automatically.
Done → CLOSE.
6
DEBRIEF — 6 tabs
Scores — CBTA Radar CPT+FO + competency bars
Exercises — Summary of each closed exercise
TEM — Threats / errors / UAS synthesis
CRM — 12 items · Pass mark 70%
Questionnaire — Trainee self-assessment
Conclusion — Summary + recommendations + PDF
7
REPORT — PDF & Email
REPORT button → full report with scores + TEM + conclusion.
✉ EMAIL button for direct sending. Archived in history.
8
END — Save & History
END button → session archived with scores, PDF report, TEM data. Find in Dashboard → History.
📂 EXERCISE LIBRARY
MEMORYImmediate memory actions — no checklist
MANEUVERSpecial maneuvers — TCAS, Windshear, GPWS, Upset…
FLIGHT PATTERNApproach procedures and standard patterns
NNCNon-Normal Checklists — system failures QRH
MANDATORYEASA mandatory — RTO, SE ILS, SE GA, SE LDG, TCAS, Windshear, Upset
LVOLow Visibility Operations — CAT II/III, LVTO, LVTO REJECT
RNPRNP and RNP AR approaches — precision navigation
💡 SMART SEARCH
Type a name (ENGINE FIRE), competency (SA, WLM), QRH page (MAN.1), or phase (TAKEOFF). Category and phase filters in real time.
🗂 SESSION LOFTS
🗂
What is a Loft?
A saved, reusable exercise list. Ex: create OPC Standard 2026 once, reload it in one tap every session.
➕
Create a Loft
🗂 button → ＋ NEW LOFT → name it → ＋ Add → select exercises → SAVE.
▶
Load a Loft
Before session: ▶ Load → exercises added at startup.
Active session: exercises added immediately.
💡 LOFT IDEAS
OPC Standard · LVO Check · Initial Type Rating · Pilot Remediation
👤 PILOT IDENTIFICATION — STAFF NUMBER
① Enter the company staff number in the dedicated field (e.g.: CA-1234).
② If the pilot is known, the badge changes from NEW to ✓ N SESSIONS and the name fills in automatically.
③ If a new pilot, enter the name manually — it will be remembered next time.
WHY THE STAFF NUMBER?
Two pilots may have the same name. The staff number ensures the correct progression file is retrieved, even months later or by another TRI.
⏱ TIMERS — SESSION · EXERCISE · TIME LIMIT
① SESSION TIMER — HH:MM:SS (manual)
Displayed top left. Does not start automatically. Press ▶ when ready.
0 → 2h00
GREENNormal
2h → 3h50
ORANGE🔔 beep 2h00
3h50 → +
REDflashing + 🔔🔔
4h00 → LEGAL LIMIT 🔔🔔🔔🔔🔔
② EXERCISE TIMER — MM:SS (automatic)
Starts automatically when exercise opens. ⏸ PAUSE and ■ STOP buttons.
Colors: white (running) · orange (paused) · grey (stopped).
③ TIME LIMIT — Configurable clock
Click --:-- on the exercise → cockpit digital clock. Adjust −10/−5/−1/+1/+5/+10 min. Customisable neon colour.
< 80% limit
GREEN
80% → 100%
ORANGE — pulsing
≥ 100%
RED — flash + halo
⏱ S / L — SAVING OR LOSS
When closing an exercise with a time limit: badge S:Xm (green = time saved) or L:Xm (red = time exceeded). Summary in Debrief → Exercises.
⏱ TIME MANAGEMENT
Per-exercise time tracking — visible in Debrief → Exercises
① ACTIVATE A TIME LIMIT
In the exercise list, tap the ⏱ button on a card → enter duration in minutes → confirm. The exercise timer starts automatically when the exercise opens.
② SAVING / LOSS AT CLOSE
When the exercise closes, a badge appears on the card:
S:Xm green = crew finished before the limit
L:Xm red = crew exceeded the limit
③ RECAP IN DEBRIEF
Debrief → Exercises tab → TIME MANAGEMENT block:
• Table per exercise: set limit + S or L in minutes
• Average per exercise — overall time management indicator
• If loss detected → automatic recommendations (briefing, checklist, coordination)
⚠ IF NO TIME LIMIT SET
The TIME MANAGEMENT block remains visible with an invitation message. Activate at least one time limit on an exercise to see the data.
📋 INSIDE AN EXERCISE — THE 4 TABS IN DETAIL
EVALUATION — Grades & Observables
Grades 1–5 per competency. Observables + / −. Switch 📋 CPT ↔ 📋 FO.
FFA — FLY · FOCUS · ACT
FLY — Aircraft secured? Position, energy, trajectory.
FOCUS — Crew managing the primary threat?
ACT — Actions adapted and prioritised?
TEM — Threat & Error Management
▲ THREATS — external events (weather, ATC, failure…)
● ERRORS — crew actions/inactions
◆ UAS — Undesired Aircraft States
Countermeasure: ✓ Managed / ~ Partial / ✗ Unmanaged. Auto timestamp.
FACILITATION — Debrief questions
Prepared questions to guide the trainee's reflection. Check off those addressed during the debrief.
🎭 TRAINING MODE — GHOST SESSION
Button at the bottom of the welcome screen. Training mode sessions are NOT saved in history. Ideal for preparing or testing a new scenario.
🎭 TRAININGCyan = inactive (normal mode)
🎭 TRAININGOrange = active (ghost session)
🎭 TRAINING MODE banner at top of session — impossible to confuse with a real session.
💾 BACKUP — JSON EXPORT & IMPORT
📤
Export
History → ⬇ Export → JSON with all sessions and pilot files. Save to iCloud / Google Drive.
📥
Import
⬆ Import → MERGE mode (adds without overwriting) or REPLACE mode.
📎
External session
📎 button → import a report from another TRI. 📎 IMPORTED badge in history.
⚠️ IMPORTANT
Cache cleared = data lost. Export after every session.
📈 HISTORY & PILOT FILE
DASHBOARD button on the welcome screen. 3 tabs:
DASHBOARD— Global KPIs, fleet competencies, latest sessions
SESSIONS— Chronological list, access to PDF reports
PILOTS— Average score, trend, XP bars, Progression button
📈 VIEW FULL FILE → all pilot sessions + PDF reports.
📈 Progression → multi-session curve + cumulative XP bars.
⚡ XP SYSTEM — CUMULATIVE PROGRESSION
XP = sum of grades across all sessions. A pilot with 15 XP in KNO has demonstrated their knowledge across multiple sessions.
🌱
ROOKIE
0–1 XP
🌿
STANDARD
2–5 XP
⭐
CONFIRMED
6–11 XP
🔥
ADVANCED
12–19 XP
⚡
EXPERT
20+ XP — multi-session mastery demonstrated
XP bars visible in each pilot file (Pilots tab) + Progression overlay.
💡 QUICK TIPS
▶SESSION timer — start it before the first exercise, not after
✓Always CLOSE an exercise for it to count in the debrief and history
⚠CRM = tab in the DEBRIEF, not in the exercise
🎲Random threat — inject without warning to test WLM/SA in real conditions
📖REF → CORE OBs — use during debrief so trainees self-identify behaviors
🪪Always enter the staff number to link the session to the correct pilot file
AFont size: A- / A / A+ / A++ buttons at the top right of the welcome screen
☀️Day/night mode — ☀️ button available on all screens
💾Export JSON after every session — it is your only external backup
🎲 THREAT INJECTOR — HOW TO USE
🎲 button in the bottom bar of an open exercise. Two modes:
🎲 RANDOM INJECTION
Tap 🎲 RANDOM INJECTION to surprise the crew with a random threat. The threat inserts simultaneously into CPT and FO TEM with automatic timestamp. No warning to trainees — ideal for testing WLM/SA reactivity.
📋 CHOOSE FROM LIST
Select a specific threat for a targeted exercise. Each threat includes a description and a pre-written TEM label.
16 THREATS IN 5 CATEGORIES
🎙 ATC — 4 threats
• Diversion clearance — unexpected radar vector toward alternate airfield
• Radio communication loss — NORDO, squawk 7600, continuation decision
• Unexpected TCAS RA — "CLIMB CLIMB" without ATC warning, mandatory RA follow
• Blocked runway — TWR "Go around, aircraft on runway", immediate go-around
🚨 CABIN — 4 threats
• Passenger medical emergency — unconscious, defibrillator, diversion decision
• Unarmed door reported — 2L on final, confirmation required before landing
• Cabin smoke — rear area, unidentified source, diversion?
• Disruptive passenger — threatening, squawk 7500 considered, ATC coordination
⛈ WEATHER — 3 threats
• Alternate unavailable — sudden fog, RVR below minima, limited fuel
• Reported windshear — PIREP windshear 400ft, 20kt loss, go-around?
• Unexpected icing — moderate to severe not forecast on ATIS, anti-ice activated
🧊 RUNWAY — 1 threat
• Runway contamination — Braking action POOR, performance not recalculated, hold or land?
⚙ SYSTEM — 4 threats
• Fuel imbalance — ECAM FUEL IMBAL increasing, balance fuel procedure
• Abnormal pressurisation — ECAM CABIN ALTITUDE, emergency descent considered?
• Cargo fire warning — ECAM FWD CARGO FIRE, NNC cargo fire, immediate diversion
• Bird strike — detected during takeoff/approach, engine check, return?
💡 TRI BEST PRACTICE
Inject without warning the crew mid-procedure. Observe: who detects first (SA), who prioritises (WLM), who communicates (COM). Record the reaction immediately in TEM before continuing. The threat is automatically timestamped.
⚠️ MANDATORY — COLORS & FULL PROCEDURE
The 9 MANDATORY EASA items appear in a coloured bar at the top of the exercise list. Each item has its own neon colour for instant identification:
RTO
Rejected Takeoff
EF V1–V2
Engine Failure V1–V2
SE ILS
Single Engine ILS
SE GO-AROUND
Single Engine Go-Around
SE LANDING
Single Engine Landing
NON-ILS
Non-ILS to Minima
TCAS RA
TCAS Resolution Advisory
WINDSHEAR
Windshear Escape Maneuver
UPSET RECOVERY
Unusual Attitude Recovery
HOW TO USE THE MANDATORY BAR
RTO Dark background, greyed text → exercise not yet completed
RTO ✅ Coloured background + ✅ → exercise closed successfully
↻ 2nd ATTEMPT — PROCEDURE
①The MANDATORY exercise is graded < 3 (below standard) and closed
②A 2nd badge and a ↻ 2nd ATTEMPT button appear in the list
③Press ↻ 2nd ATTEMPT → a duplicate exercise is added to the list
④Redo the exercise → close it → the 2nd attempt is graded separately in the debrief
⚠ EASA REGULATION
All 9 MANDATORY items must be completed and closed to validate an EASA OPC/PC. One unclosed item = check not valid under regulations. The MANDATORY bar gives you real-time status.
⚡ FORDEC
Decision-making model — use it when facing any abnormal situation
F
FACTS
What is happening? Gather and share the available information.
O
OPTIONS
What alternatives exist? List without judging, involve the crew.
R
RISKS & BENEFITS
Risks and benefits of each option. Which one is best?
D
DECISION
Decision made and announced. Crew aligned on the action plan.
E
EXECUTION
Who does what? Role allocation and procedure execution.
C
CHECK
Did the action produce the expected result? Confirm or reconsider.
IN THE APP — FORDEC Tab
Open an exercise → FORDEC tab → check FORDEC USED if the crew applied the method. A F badge appears on the card and the debrief displays the usage rate.
📡 NITS BRIEF
Crew brief — done AFTER FORDEC, BEFORE executing the procedure
N
NATURE
Nature of the failure or abnormal situation detected.
I
INTENTION
What the PIC intends to do — the chosen procedure and its objective.
T
TIME
Time available or execution moment — urgency or time to think?
S
SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS
Special instructions, precautions, specific PF/PM roles.
COMPLETE SEQUENCE
Failure detected → FORDEC (decide) → NITS (brief the crew) → Execute the procedure. A N badge appears on the card if the NITS brief was completed.
📚 COMPLETE SOURCES & REFERENCES
COLOR CODE: ICAO EASA BOEING CRM/METHODS SAFETY DATA OPS DOCS
ICAO — INTERNATIONAL REGULATION
ICAODoc 9995 — CBTA Manual
Normative framework for the 9 CBTA competencies, definitions and observable behaviors. Basis for all assessment in this app.
ICAODoc 9683 — Human Factors Training Manual
TEM framework (Threat & Error Management) — threats, errors and undesired aircraft states. Basis for the LOSA model.
EASA — EUROPEAN REGULATION
EASAPart-FCL Appendix 9
Regulatory requirements for OPC, PC and TRI Checks. Mandatory exercises, frequencies and validation criteria.
EASAAMC/GM to Part-FCL — CBTA Grading Standards
Official scale 1→5 (UNACCEPTABLE → EXEMPLARY), competency assessment criteria and behavioral indicators.
EASAORO.FC.115 — TRI/SFI Qualification
Requirements for simulator instructors. Qualification maintenance, oversight and assessment responsibility.
EASANOTECHS — Non-Technical Skills Framework
12 CRM behavioral items assessed in OPC/PC. NOTECHS pass mark: 70%. Integrated in the app's CRM calculation.
BOEING / IATA — TECHNICAL DOCUMENTS
BOEINGNMS CBTA TEM Pilot Observer Document
Official Boeing reference for CBTA observers. Observation grids and behavioral indicators per competency.
BOEINGFCT 737 NG — Flight Crew Training
Demonstrated XW limits (TALPA/RCC), RCAM, LVO procedures. Source of all weather limits in the app. * 36/33 kt with winglets.
BOEINGQRH Rev 55 — Quick Reference Handbook
Memory items, checklists and B737 NG limitations. Basis for NNC exercises and mandatory items.
BOEINGFCTM Rev 25 — Flight Crew Training Manual
Piloting techniques, procedures philosophy and Boeing B737 NG best practices.
BOEING/IATAEBT — Evidence-Based Training
Scenario-building methodology from real data (accidents, FOQA, ASAP). The app's behavioral indicators are derived from this.
CRM & TRAINING METHODS
CRMFORDEC — Lufthansa Aviation Training
Facts · Options · Risks & Benefits · Decision · Execution · Check. Crew decision-making standard adopted globally in commercial aviation.
CRMNITS — Commercial Aviation Briefing Standard
Nature · Intention · Time · Special Instructions. Structured crew briefing model, industry standard.
MODELTEM — Threat & Error Management (ICAO)
Pre-flight threat identification, crew error management and UAS recovery. App TEM tab.
MODELFFA — Fly, Focus, Act (ICAO)
ICAO situational awareness filter: Fly → Focus → Act prioritization. Integrated in each exercise's Evaluation tab.
SAFETY DATA & ANALYSIS
SAFETYFOQA — Flight Operations Quality Assurance
Flight recorder data analyzed to identify recurring deviations. Source of high-risk EBT scenarios.
SAFETYASAP — Aviation Safety Action Program
Voluntary crew incident reports. Complements FOQA by capturing human factors not visible on flight recorders.
ICAOLOSA — Line Operations Safety Audit
Real-line safety audit (ICAO). Identifies threats and errors under normal operating conditions. Feeds the TNA analysis.
OPERATIONAL DOCUMENTS
SOP
Standard Operating Procedures — B737 NG
NNC
Non-Normal Checklists — B737 NG QRH
AOM
Aircraft Operating Manual
OM-D
Operations Manual — Section D (operator)
⚖️ REGULATORY DISCLAIMER
This application is a TRI support tool — it does not replace the official Boeing CBTA Package, QRH, FCTM or your operator's OM-D. All B737-specific content must be validated by the TRI against documents approved by the competent authority before operational use. Responsibility for the assessment remains with the instructor.
By Cpt Bouchenafa Mohamed Amine · B737NG QRH Rev55 · FCTM Rev25
📖 RÉFÉRENCE BOEING CBTA
TEM Card · Core Competencies OBs · 🔍 Pincez pour zoomer
📺 DATA SHOW
Reset session
Effacer tous les exercices ?
📎 IMPORTER SESSION EXTERNE
Rapport d'un autre instructeur
COMMANDANT DE BORD
PREMIER OFFICIER (optionnel)
SCORES CPT PAR COMPÉTENCE (optionnel · 0 = non évalué)
📎 RAPPORT PDF ORIGINAL (optionnel)
Aucun fichier sélectionné
Stocké localement · Taille max recommandée : 5 Mo
Fin de session
Terminer la session et revenir à l'accueil ?
Les données non exportées seront perdues.
Modifier la session
